site_analysis_review.platform_notes: Site analysis says 'custom' platform. The URL pattern /{slug}/{id}.html and fully JS-rendered page with JSON-LD suggests a custom/spa framework, possibly Adobe Commerce (Magento) or similar enterprise solution. No definitive platform markers found in probe data.
site_analysis_review.anti_bot_notes: Site analysis says 'detected: true, severity: medium' which is misleading. Standard Playwright and direct HTTP ARE blocked, but this is more of a 'browser gating' behavior than traditional anti-bot. When UC Chrome with datacenter proxy is used, there is NO anti-bot detection, NO CAPTCHA. The site simply requires a realistic browser fingerprint. Severity should be 'low' once UC Chrome is used — it works cleanly on first attempt with datacenter proxy.
site_analysis_review.mechanism_notes: Site analysis recommends seleniumbase_uc which is correct since UC Chrome is the only method that works. However, the extraction method can be simplified: JSON-LD has complete product data including offers with price. The scraper does NOT need to parse DOM CSS selectors (all return NOT FOUND). It only needs to extract and parse the JSON-LD script blocks from the rendered HTML.
site_analysis_review.extraction_notes: Site analysis correctly identifies JSON-LD as primary method and correctly notes that CSS selectors are unreliable. JSON-LD has offers=present so price is extractable from structured data.
corrections: Anti-bot severity: site_analyzer says 'medium', actual is 'low' (UC Chrome works without challenges)
Anti-bot detected: site_analyzer says 'true', probe says 'false' when using correct method (UC Chrome)
Primary extraction should emphasize JSON-LD-only approach since ALL CSS selectors are NOT FOUND
No Open Graph tags present (site analysis didn't explicitly note this)

connectivity.notes: Standard Playwright MCP and direct HTTP are blocked. UC Chrome with datacenter proxy is the ONLY method that works. No CAPTCHA on successful connection. The page content (50K+ chars) is fully JS-rendered.

extraction_methods.notes: JSON-LD Product block contains all essential fields including offers with price. No OG tags available. All common CSS selectors (h1, price, sku, availability, rating, brand, description) return NOT FOUND on the rendered page. Extraction should rely entirely on JSON-LD parsing from rendered HTML.

url.notes: Extract from JSON-LD Product.url. Fallback to the URL being scraped.

src_url.notes: Set from the input_urls.json entry. Not extracted from page.

status_code.notes: HTTP status code from the response. Set by scraper, not extracted from page.

scraped_at.notes: ISO-8601 timestamp set by scraper at time of extraction.

remarks.notes: Set by scraper for any warnings or notes about the extraction.

title.notes: h1 NOT FOUND in DOM. Extract from JSON-LD Product.name. Page title also confirms: 'C15 Super Booster | Brightening Booster | Paula's Choice'

price.notes: JSON-LD offers=present meaning price data IS in structured data. All CSS price selectors return NOT FOUND. Extract from JSON-LD.

availability.notes: Extract from JSON-LD offers.availability. The value is a schema.org URL that needs mapping to human-readable status.

original_price.notes: May be available if multiple offer items exist in JSON-LD or if highPrice/lowPrice are set. Needs verification against actual JSON-LD content.

description.notes: Available in JSON-LD Product.description. DOM description selector also NOT FOUND.

mpn.notes: Available in JSON-LD Product.mpn (Manufacturer Part Number)

reviews.notes: Individual reviews available in JSON-LD Product.review array

location.notes: No location/warehouse data found in JSON-LD or page structure.

category.notes: May be present in JSON-LD. BreadcrumbList block not detected (only Product and FAQPage found).

block_1.offers_notes: Offers block contains price data. Price IS extractable from JSON-LD.

block_2.notes: FAQPage block with Q&A content. Not useful for product data extraction.

variants.notes: No variant selector information available from probe data. The product page may have size options but variant structure was not testable through probe. JSON-LD may contain multiple offers for variants if present.

page_structure.title: C15 Super Booster | Brightening Booster | Paula's Choice
page_structure.notes: Page is fully JavaScript-rendered. All common CSS selectors (h1, price, sku, availability, brand, description, rating) return NOT FOUND. The only reliable extraction path is JSON-LD structured data from the rendered HTML.

mechanism_reassessment.justification: Confirmed correct. Only UC Chrome with datacenter proxy can access the page. Standard Playwright and direct HTTP are blocked. JSON-LD extraction from rendered HTML is the optimal approach — no need for complex DOM CSS selector parsing since all common selectors are NOT FOUND. The scraper workflow should be: 1) Load page with UC Chrome, 2) Parse all script[type=application/ld+json] blocks, 3) Extract Product block fields, 4) Map availability URL to text, 5) Done.
mechanism_reassessment.extraction_strategy_notes: Since all CSS selectors are NOT FOUND and JSON-LD has complete data including offers with price, the scraper should extract ONLY from JSON-LD. This simplifies the code significantly — no fragile CSS selector maintenance needed. Just parse the JSON-LD Product block.

confidence_notes: High confidence on field mapping via JSON-LD (structured_data_available=true, offers_present=true). Medium confidence on exact field values since Playwright MCP was blocked and I couldn't inspect individual JSON-LD values. The field list is comprehensive based on probe-reported JSON-LD keys. Connectivity is verified (uc_chrome_datacenter works).
